# Working basic test that doesn't require external tools

# Test 1: Basic echo functionality
exec echo "working test started"
stdout 'working test started'

# Test 2: Test that go is available
exec go version
stdout 'go version'

# Test 3: Simple file operations
# File content is provided in txtar format at the end

# Test 4: File reading
exec cat test.json
stdout '"test": "basic"'
stdout '"status": "working"'

# Test 5: Basic shell operations
bash 'echo "shell test: $(echo success)"'
stdout 'shell test: success'

# Test 6: Environment variables
env TEST_VAR=working
bash 'echo "env test: $TEST_VAR"'
stdout 'env test: working'

# Test 7: Simple JSON processing (using basic tools)
bash 'echo "{\\"rapid\\": true}" | grep -q "rapid" && echo "json test passed"'
stdout 'json test passed'

# Test 8: File operations
bash 'ls *.json | head -1'
stdout 'test.json'

# Test 9: Multiple operations
bash 'touch temp.txt && echo "temp created" && rm temp.txt'
stdout 'temp created'

# Test 10: Cleanup and completion
exec rm -f test.json
exec echo "working basic test completed"
stdout 'working basic test completed'

-- test.json --
{"test": "basic", "status": "working"}
